---
title: "Agentic-Coding Evaluation Lab"
---

This is the public methodology site for the Agentic-Coding Evaluation Lab: an
evidence-gated way to decide whether to change a coding-agent default (which
model, or which model-plus-harness combination, to deploy). A candidate
may replace the incumbent only after a registered, reproducible evaluation
clears an independently recomputable, fail-closed scientific rule. The released
0.2.0 package preserves the project's historical rule and worked examples; it
is not authorization for a new powered claim. The successor estimand, test,
replicate policy, and practical-benefit threshold remain under review. The
single strongest result is a defensible refusal to overclaim, not a speedup. No
performance or alignment improvement deltas are claimed anywhere here.

The full write-up lives in the repository
[README](https://github.com/verlyn13/eval-lab-methodology). This site carries
the normative specifications, experimental methods review, and rendered
example reports.

::: {.callout-important title="Current scientific result"}
Exact arithmetic makes a calculation reproducible; it does not, by itself,
make the inference exact. In the current synthetic study, one bit-exact
sign-flip construction exceeds its nominal one-sided 5% level under part of the
tested composite-null boundary. The alternative calibrated construction is
defined only for a common i.i.d. trinomial model on a finite nuisance grid and
has not been validated for the intended class-balanced task design. No
successor method is selected.

[Read the executable experimental inference note](reports/experimental-inference-note.qmd).
:::

## The released methodology in brief

The bullets below describe the historical 0.2.0 core and synthetic examples.
They remain reproducible records, but they are not the analysis plan for a new
powered campaign.

- **Two measurement axes.** Reliability evaluations ask whether the agent can
  do the plumbing correctly and repeatably; capability evaluations ask whether
  it can accomplish task-class work at all. The axes trade against each other,
  so they are never averaged into one number.
- **Calibrated small-n statistics.** Wilson score intervals for proportions,
  seeded bootstrap confidence intervals for paired deltas (2000 iterations,
  fixed seed, exactly reproducible), and a two-sided paired sign test reported
  alongside — never as the gate.
- **A conjunctive, fail-closed promotion gate.** A candidate is promoted only
  if every criterion holds; a criterion that cannot be evaluated is not a
  pass. The gate is deliberately biased toward the incumbent because the costs
  are asymmetric.
- **Identity-domain comparability.** Runs are score-comparable only when they
  come from the same [identity domain](identity-domain.qmd) — a structural
  fingerprint of everything that can change numerics. Comparisons across
  domains fail closed unless a bridge authorization is recorded.
- **Evidence, not assertions.** Every published interval is recomputable from
  a versioned evidence JSON that carries raw per-task outcomes and a
  reproducibility manifest.

The next contract raises this bar: the public methodology implementation must
derive the enforcing result and decision from raw records and refuse a producer
claim that does not match exactly. Immutable draft `2.0.0-draft.1` now
demonstrates that structural path on public synthetic records. It remains
repository-only, unreleased, and operationally non-authoritative. Because the
inferential basis and test, replication policy, and practical-benefit threshold
remain undecided, its only valid outcome is `NOT_EVALUABLE`.

## Specifications

- [Identity domains](identity-domain.qmd) — the normative comparability spec,
  the canonical field set and hashing rule, and the frozen cross-repo
  conformance vector (recomputed live when this page renders).
- [Experimental inference note](reports/experimental-inference-note.qmd) —
  executable synthetic decision input showing where exact computation does and
  does not establish inferential validity. It selects no method.
- [Contract B v2 refusal example](reports/contract-v2-not-evaluable.qmd) —
  a synthetic registration-to-report join that independently recomputes or
  refuses and cannot emit a promotion recommendation.

## Example reports

Rendered from public evidence JSON by the parameterized Quarto report in this
repository. All data below is **synthetic** — labeled as such in each
artifact's manifest — and exists to demonstrate the reporting layer, not to
claim results.

- [Sample synthetic lab report](reports/sample/methodology-report.html)
- [Campaign: synthetic-local-promotion-001](reports/campaigns/synthetic-local-promotion-001/methodology-report.html)

## Honesty rules

Every number published by this project traces to a produced artifact.
Synthetic and representative data are labeled as synthetic or representative.
Enforcing, advisory, and planned mechanisms are labeled as such, and the
limitations (small n, wide intervals, confounds) are stated plainly — the
honesty is the point of the artifact.
